comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report, 1:48 PM

Good Afternoon, Sugarloafers,

Sometimes, something as simple as a blue sky is enough to make us smile. And that's what it did today. With blue skies and the snow on the trees, it was a picturesque day.

Groomers are going to be busy tonight. I know our trail count dropped a smidge today, but with another pass of the tiller tonight, things should really soften up, and we'll be back in action tomorrow. Groomers will work both shifts and groom everything, including Skidder, Narrow Gauge, Haywire, Tote, and more.

The cats should have an easier time tonight breaking up the snow, but there is still a potential for a grooming delay in the King Pine area in the morning. When we chat tomorrow, I'll give you an update.

Lifts for tomorrow are West Mountain and Snubber at 8 AM. After that, it's DRC-B, Sawduster, Skyline, Bucksaw, Skidway, Whiffletree, King Pine, and Superquad at 8:30 AM. Moosecalator begins to spin at 9 AM.

Superquad will be running until 4:20 PM tomorrow.

Temperatures are dropping tonight, and it will feel chilly tomorrow. We might be the king of spring, but it's still winter at the top of the mountain. Temps will be in the low teens at the summit and the low twenties at the base. It might be a bit breezy in the morning but it shouldn't be enough to impact the lifts.

Quick reminder that Widowmaker will have modified après hours of 1:30 to 4:30 PM today, because it's closing at 4:30 for a private event. Be sure to stop by to see Chase Winn Band before then.

Head on over to Sugarloaf Board Room and Downhill Supply Co. for their huge sales, with gear up to 40% off across some of your favorite brands.

Tomorrow is the last day of Spring Demo Days, so swing by the Sugarshack Demo Center to test the latest in ski technology. Tomorrow is Blizzard / Kastle Demo Day, where all Blizzard / Kastle-branded skis are $20 to demo. All other demos are $75. Check out the info in our quick links.

Stay stoked,
Shannon
